title: The Goal: Feature Density
concept: Feature density is the goal of packaging the maximum amount of predictive signal into the smallest possible number of features.

exercises.prompt: Warmup: What is the main disadvantage of using wrapper methods for feature selection?

options.text: They are computationally slow because they repeatedly train models
They are incapable of finding interactions between features
They require converting all numerical data back to text

exercises.explain: Correct! Wrapper methods are slow because they train models on many combinations. Now, let's look at the ultimate goal of feature selection!

cards.title: Concentrated fruit juice
cards.body: If you buy orange juice, you can buy a giant carton of mostly water, or a small can of frozen concentrate. The concentrate has the same flavor but takes up way less space. In ML, we want concentrated data — high signal in a tiny package.

cards.title: Feature Density
cards.body: Feature Density refers to the ratio of useful predictive signal to the number of columns in your dataset. High feature density means every single column is packed with unique, high-value information, leaving no room for noise.

cards.title: The elite sports team
cards.body: Think of your feature list like a sports team. A roster of 100 mediocre players is hard to manage and underperforms. An elite team of 5 superstars is efficient, highly coordinated, and wins games. Feature selection is about cuts: keeping only the superstars.

cards.title: Simplifying a health model
cards.body: A model predicting diabetes risk with 100 features might get 85% accuracy. By selecting only the top 5 features (like age, BMI, and blood sugar), we might get 84.5% accuracy. We traded 0.5% accuracy for a model that is 20 times faster and easier to deploy.

cards.title: Maximum accuracy isn't the only goal
cards.body: Beginners often think the goal of ML is 100% accuracy at all costs. In production, a slightly less accurate model that uses 90% fewer features is almost always preferred because it is cheaper to run, faster, and much less likely to break.

cards.title: Sparse vs. Dense features
cards.body: Comparing the efficiency of feature choices:

left.text:
150 columns of noisy data
Slow, fragile, hard to debug

right.text:
6 high-signal columns
Fast, robust, easy to maintain

options.text: Your dataset has a small number of features, each containing high predictive value
Your dataset has thousands of columns, regardless of their quality
Every row in your dataset contains the exact same numbers
The model runs slower but uses more memory

exercises.explain: High feature density means you have concentrated signal in a minimal number of columns, avoiding bloat.

exercises.prompt: True or false: A model with 5 high-signal features is usually preferred in production over a model with 100 features that is only 0.1% more accurate.
exercises.explain: True! In the real world, simpler models are faster, cheaper, and easier to maintain, making them highly preferred.

exercises.sentence: Maximizing the ratio of predictive signal to the number of columns is called increasing feature ___.
exercises.explain: Feature density is the core objective of feature selection: packing maximum signal into minimum dimensions.

exercises.prompt: Which model exhibits the highest feature density?

options.text: A model using 3 features to achieve 92% accuracy
A model using 80 features to achieve 92% accuracy
A model using 500 features to achieve 80% accuracy

exercises.explain: The first model packs the same accuracy (92%) into only 3 features, making its data highly dense and efficient.

exercises.prompt: You built a housing price model with 40 features that achieves 91% accuracy. After applying variance thresholding and correlation filtering, you select the top 6 features. The new model gets 90.5% accuracy. What is your best course of action for production?

options.text: Use the 6-feature model because it is far simpler, faster, and more robust with virtually no loss in accuracy
Use the 40-feature model to chase the extra 0.5% accuracy, ignoring speed and complexity
Scrap both models and collect 1,000 more features to try to reach 100% accuracy
Manually average the predictions of both models together

exercises.explain: Magnificent! The trade-off is absolutely worth it. A 6-feature model is much easier to maintain, faster to run, and less prone to breaking. You've mastered the fundamentals of feature selection! Ready for the next unit where we learn how to create entirely new features from existing ones!
